_comment: Source of truth for the Alex-call roadmap tickets. Edit here, then run scripts/create-github-issues.sh to push to GitHub Issues. Plan rationale lives in .claude/plans/alex-call-roadmap.md (referenced per ticket).

milestones.title: Sprint 1 — Trust & Stability
milestones.description: Honest, predictable, crash-free under real investigative load.

milestones.title: Sprint 2 — Discovery & Flow
milestones.description: Find anything across all phones/types; graph shows who initiated what; author nodes anywhere.

milestones.title: Sprint 3 — Deliverables & Depth
milestones.description: Turn analysis into client-ready output; reliable access to the full historical record.

tickets.title: Bug-scrub session + triage board
tickets.body: Walk every Cellebrite tab with a 2-phone, ~230K-event case loaded. Log each crash/error/empty-silent-state into a triaged list (sev 1–3). Deliverable is the triaged list; later tasks reference its IDs. No code.

tickets.title: Invalid-Date hardening audit
tickets.body: Grep all `toISOString()` / `new Date(` call sites in `frontend/src/components/cellebrite/**`. Confirm each guards NaN. Known-good guards: `CellebriteTimeline.jsx:319-322`, `timeline.js:143-148`. Fix any unguarded site found in S1-01.

tickets.title: Empty/error states across all tabs
tickets.body: Every tab must render a clear state for (a) no phones selected, (b) zero results, (c) fetch error with retry. `NoPhonesSelectedEmptyState.jsx` exists — ensure it (or equivalent) is mounted in Comms, Events, Timeline, Graph, Files.

tickets.title: Load-under-pressure check
tickets.body: Verify the per-type 5,000 cap (`CellebriteTimeline.jsx:187`, `cellebrite.py:690`) never lets the UI attempt a full 230K render. Confirm the honest truncation notice (`CellebriteTimeline.jsx:396-407`) fires. Document the safe ceiling.

tickets.title: Sort-direction toggle (older→newer / newer→older)
tickets.body: Add a control to `CellebriteTimeline.jsx`. Today sort is hard-coded DESC at `:293-297`. Make direction state-driven (default DESC), persisted per-case in localStorage. Day-group ordering and intra-day row order both follow the toggle.

tickets.title: Full date + time on rows
tickets.body: Row currently shows time-only (`CellebriteTimeline.jsx:682` slices off the date). Show full `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` via `formatTs` when rows aren't under an obvious day header (flat/search results); keep time-only under day headers.

tickets.title: Timezone-offset chip on timestamps
tickets.body: Surface the active zone offset (e.g. `UTC−4`) next to timestamps, sourced from the zone engine (`cellebriteTime.js:100-106`). Selector already shows it globally (`CellebriteTimezone.jsx:84-87`); add an inline per-row/section affordance. Display only — no new tz logic.

tickets.title: Working timezone applies to filters, not just display
tickets.body: Confirm date-range filtering operates in the selected zone end-to-end (grouping already does via `tzDayKey`). If the date-range inputs/scrubber convert to UTC before filtering, align them to the selected zone so a 'Nov 4' filter means local Nov 4. Spike first (is the filter path zone-aware?), then fix if needed.

tickets.title: Make the zone selector discoverable per case
tickets.body: Ensure the `CellebriteTimezone` selector is visible in Timeline + Event Center headers (not buried). Default to device zone; offer UTC.

tickets.title: Sender = device owner name when phone is sender
tickets.body: In `CommsMessageBubble.jsx:145-165` verify owner-as-sender renders the owner's name (currently 'You' at `:154`); decide with Alex whether owner shows as real name vs 'You' in cross-phone context.

tickets.title: Recipient shows the on-device saved contact name
tickets.body: Recipient label should use the contact name as stored on the sending device, not a global canonical name. Trace recipient field `commsUtils.js` → bubble. Spike to confirm backend exposes the device-local alias; file a backend follow-up if not.

tickets.title: Show both phone numbers
tickets.body: On message/call rows and detail, display both parties' raw numbers alongside names. `PersonName.jsx` is the formatting choke point.

tickets.title: To/From numbers inline on the timeline row
tickets.body: Row builds `sender → recipient` from names only (`CellebriteTimeline.jsx:683-691`); recipient falls back `counterpart` → `recipients[0]`. Render the number beside each name (`Name · +1 555…`) for both parties on one truncating line; full values in `title`/flyout. Reuse `PersonName.jsx`. Spike S1-13a: confirm the events payload carries `identifier`/number; if not, add to the lean projection in `neo4j_service`.

tickets.title: Variable-height virtualized rows (prerequisite for inline media)
tickets.body: Convert the windowed list (`:673`, `timelineRowHeight`, `bottomPad`) from fixed-height to measured per-index heights (measure-and-cache keyed by event id; recompute offsets). Load-bearing for inline body/media/player. Spike S1-14a: validate scroll stability + perf with ~5–10K mixed-height rows (jump-to-day, scroll restore, fast fling).

tickets.title: Inline message body (no click)
tickets.body: Render the message text/body directly in the row (not just `ev.summary`). Clamp to N lines with a 'more' affordance so height stays bounded (measured-height system absorbs variation). Read while scrolling, zero clicks. Depends on S1-14.

tickets.title: Inline image thumbnails (no click)
tickets.body: Show actual image thumbnails inline (`CommsMediaStrip` already renders up to MAX_THUMBS=3 in the non-expandable branch `:75-90`/`:114-122`). Lazy-load on viewport entry so thousands of rows don't fire thousands of requests. Click a thumb → full-size lightbox (additive). Depends on S1-14.

tickets.title: Inline voice-note player (play from the row, no expand)
tickets.body: Render an inline audio player for the voice kind (`CommsMediaStrip.jsx:31`) so the investigator plays the voicenote straight from the timeline row without opening/expanding. Reuse `CommsAttachment`'s audio renderer in a compact inline form, alongside any other media for that message. Lazy-init on viewport entry; fetch audio bytes only on play. Depends on S1-14.

tickets.title: Density & performance tuning
tickets.body: With body + thumbnails + inline player + To/From numbers visible, keep rows scannable: bounded default height, line-clamped body, consistent grid. Verify measured-height scroller stays smooth at ~230K rows (visible window only; media lazy). Confirm jump-to-day from the scrubber lands correctly with variable heights.

tickets.title: Discovery shell (search all phones + all types)
tickets.body: New top-level tab `CellebriteDiscovery.jsx` querying all phones + all data types at once. Reuse `utils/cellebriteSearch.js` + `CellebriteSearchInput`. Backed by `/comms/messages/search` (`cellebrite.py:624-650`), `/events` (`:681`), `/cross-phone-graph/search` (`:232`).

tickets.title: Result grouping + type filter
tickets.body: Results grouped by type (comms / events / locations / files / people) with multi-select type filter. Each result row shows a phone identity chip (`PhoneIdentityChip.jsx`).

tickets.title: Pivot-to-view with carried filter (shared pivot bus)
tickets.body: Clicking a result opens the right view (Timeline/Comms/Graph/Files) with the matched entity/term pre-applied as a filter. Generalize the existing 'Filter Comms' intent path (`CellebriteCommsCenter.jsx:226-246`) into a small shared 'pivot intent' bus so any result can drive any view.

tickets.title: Phrase search reaches the graph
tickets.body: Confirm a phrase like "Piney Bench Road" surfaces a usable node result and offers 'open in graph' + 'open in timeline'. Ties to Epic 2C.

tickets.title: Graph bug: search rebuilds the graph
tickets.body: Bug: 'search not rebuilding graph'. In `CellebriteCrossPhoneGraph.jsx` (tokeniser `:44-60`, filter path ~`:571`), ensure Filter-mode search re-derives the rendered node/link set and re-runs the force layout instead of only highlighting. Repro with "Piney Bench Road".

tickets.title: Graph bug: selection visibly reflects on canvas
tickets.body: Bug: 'selected not showing'. Ensure a selected node/result is visually emphasized (ring/scale/center) on the react-force-graph-2d canvas; verify the camera-preserve effect (`:216-223`) isn't swallowing the highlight.

tickets.title: Graph bug: filters actually narrow the graph
tickets.body: Bug: 'filters not working'. Audit the 14 edge-type toggles (`GRAPH_EVENT_TYPES :86-111`) and phone filter; confirm each toggle re-derives links/nodes. Add a 'no matches' empty state.

tickets.title: Define 'complete graph search' (spike → spec)
tickets.body: Enumerate what should be searchable: node labels, aliases, phone numbers/identifiers, edge types & counts, location names/addresses, app names, free text on connected events. Decide ranking and whether search runs server-side (to reach beyond the 200-node render cap) vs the current client tokeniser. Output: short spec.

tickets.title: Server-backed graph search endpoint
tickets.body: Extend `/cross-phone-graph/search` (`cellebrite.py:232-251`) so a query returns matching nodes + the subgraph needed to show them (matched node + immediate neighbours + connecting edges), independent of the default render cap. Surfaces a node not in the rendered 200 and pulls it + context into view.

tickets.title: Search across edges/attributes/numbers
tickets.body: Match on relationship type, identifiers/numbers, location addresses, and app — not just node label. Wire results so "Piney Bench Road" resolves the place node and its connected people/edges.

tickets.title: Search → rebuild + frame + select (+ expand neighbours)
tickets.body: A search result rebuilds the graph to the matched subgraph, frames it (camera fit), and applies the visible selection from S2-06. Provide 'expand neighbours' to grow outward one hop at a time (keeps the canvas legible vs dumping 200).

tickets.title: Pivot from a graph result
tickets.body: From a found node/edge, 'open in Timeline' / 'open in Comms' with the entity pre-filtered (uses the S2-03 pivot bus). Closes the find → investigate loop.

tickets.title: Backend: per-edge direction + counts (spike → schema)
tickets.body: Spike S2-17a: confirm whether `neo4j_service.get_cellebrite_cross_phone_graph` (`cellebrite.py:254-298`) can emit per comms/call edge an initiator + directional counts (A→B, B→A) and time span. Underlying data already has direction (`CommsMessageBubble.jsx:145-165`). Output edge schema: `{from, to, dir_counts:{ab,ba}, first_ts, last_ts, initiator}`.

tickets.title: Directed edge rendering (arrows / particles)
tickets.body: With direction data, render arrowheads (`linkDirectionalArrowLength`) source→target and optionally animated `linkDirectionalParticles`. For two-way comms, show a dominant-direction arrow plus a balance indicator (edge thickness or A↔B count label).

tickets.title: Flow emphasis & asymmetry
tickets.body: Encode volume/asymmetry: edge width ∝ total count; particle density/colour ∝ which party sent more. An investigator sees 'X overwhelmingly messages Y, rarely reverse' at a glance — a communication trajectory, not a static link.

tickets.title: Direction legend + connection/flow view toggle
tickets.body: Legend explaining arrows/particles; toggles to show/hide directional particles and to switch between 'connection view' (undirected, current) and 'flow view' (directed). Default keeps current view; flow view is opt-in so we don't regress familiar behaviour.

tickets.title: (Stretch) Flow over time
tickets.body: Tie the graph to a time window (reuse the scrubber concept) so direction/volume animates across the case period — chains of activity across phones become visible. Stretch; spike feasibility against the force-graph perf budget before committing.

tickets.title: Multi-select communication types
tickets.body: In `CellebriteCommsCenter.jsx` extend `CommsTypeFilter.jsx` from one-at-a-time to multi-select (call + message + email combinable). Backend already returns mixed types via `/comms/between` (`:524-577`).

tickets.title: Combine filters (type × participant × app × attachment)
tickets.body: Ensure participant filter (`:45-105`), `CommsAppFilter.jsx`, and `AttachmentFilterToggle.jsx` AND together cleanly in both 'split' and 'any' participant modes.

tickets.title: Audit + provenance spike (platform-wide node creation)
tickets.body: Node creation ALREADY exists in the main system (not Cellebrite-specific): `GraphView.jsx:1347` 'Add Node' -> `AddNodeModal.jsx` -> `POST /api/graph/create-node` (`graph.py:140`); `GraphTableView.jsx:9` '+ Add Node' uses the same endpoint; a separate path is `EntityEditorModal.jsx` -> `POST /api/case-profiles/`. Reconcile the two paths (which is canonical for 'add a node to a graph') and decide a shared provenance/audit shape (`user_created:true`, created_by, created_at) so authored nodes are distinct from imported data on EVERY graph (main, Cellebrite, v2). Output: short design note + the single dialog/endpoint to standardise on. No new core endpoint expected.

tickets.title: Create node in the Cellebrite Cross-Phone Graph
tickets.body: Add the missing 'Add node' affordance to `CellebriteCrossPhoneGraph.jsx` (today it has none), routing through the standardised dialog/endpoint from S2-24; user sets label/type, optionally links to existing nodes.

tickets.title: Create node in main graph (verify) + v2 GraphCanvas (add)
tickets.body: Main `GraphView.jsx` already has Add Node — verify it conforms to the S2-24 provenance shape. Add the affordance to v2 `frontend_v2/src/features/graph/components/GraphCanvas.tsx`, which has none yet. Promote/reuse a shared creation component so all graphs share one dialog rather than divergent modals.

tickets.title: Create node/row parity across tables
tickets.body: `GraphTableView.jsx` already has '+ Add Node'; extend the same affordance to the Cellebrite tables (`EventsTable.jsx`, unified contacts, overview tables) routing through the same dialog + endpoint, so authored entities appear consistently in tables and graphs across the platform.

tickets.title: Provenance + audit on the create-node path
tickets.body: Ensure the existing `POST /api/graph/create-node` (`graph.py:140`) persists provenance (`user_created`, created_by, created_at) and that every graph renders user-authored nodes with a clear visual distinction from imported data. Extend the endpoint only if the fields aren't already captured. Signed off by user — build approved.

tickets.title: Decide load model (Load more vs infinite scroll)
tickets.body: Confirm with Alex: explicit 'Load more' button (handwritten ask) vs infinite scroll. Default to 'Load more' (predictable, less jank on 230K sets).

tickets.title: Paginated event loading in Timeline
tickets.body: `/events` already supports `offset` (`cellebrite.py:691`); envelope (`:755-790`) gives the true total. Replace the silent per-type 5,000 cap with progressive loading: load first page, show 'Showing N of TOTAL — Load more', append on click. Keep dedupe (`CellebriteTimeline.jsx:196-198`).

tickets.title: Keyset pagination for deep pages (backend)
tickets.body: Offset pagination re-reads rows at depth. `/comms/between` already has cursor/keyset (`cellebrite.py:546-552`). Spike → add cursor pagination to `/events` so paging into ~230K events stays fast. Backend task in `neo4j_service`.

tickets.title: Apply 'Load more' to other capped lists
tickets.body: Comms threads (200, `:467`), thread detail (500, `:506`), files (500, `:1110`) — give each a consistent 'Load more' affordance using the same pattern/component.

tickets.title: Report export spike (format + generation)
tickets.body: No export today — `CellebriteReport.jsx:18-202` is a read-only device profile. Decide format (PDF/DOCX/printable HTML) and generation location (client print-to-PDF vs backend render). Output: recommendation.

tickets.title: Entity summaries
tickets.body: Generate per-entity summary blocks (who, devices present on, contact/message/call counts, activity window, aliases) building on data already in `CellebriteReport.jsx` and unified contacts. Reusable `EntitySummaryCard.jsx`.

tickets.title: Timeline callouts (annotate key events)
tickets.body: Let investigators mark/annotate key events and pull them into a 'callouts' section for the report. Persist annotations (small backend write). Signed off by user — build approved.

tickets.title: Investigation-context visualizations (export-friendly)
tickets.body: Export-friendly versions of the graph + a condensed timeline suitable for a client deliverable (clean styling, legend, no debug chrome).

tickets.title: Assemble client-facing report
tickets.body: Compose selected entity summaries + callouts + visualizations into a single client-facing document. Build signed off by user. Note: surface the actual export/download/email-out step for explicit approval at run time (per-action confirm, not a design gate).

tickets.title: Timeline media lightbox polish
tickets.body: Full-size lightbox for clicked inline thumbnails (Epic 1E renders previews inline; this is the click-to-enlarge layer). Reuse `CommsAttachment` viewer. Confirm voice + media coexist.
